title: General Verbs Related to Health and Sickness

sense.definition: to physically cause harm to a person or thing

examples.x: A misstep on the stairs could injure your leg.
Be careful with fireworks; they can injure you.
He injured his knee while playing soccer.
He was practicing gymnastics and injured his neck.
The horse kicked and injured the farmer.

sense.definition: to spread or transfer cancer cells from the original site to other parts of the body, leading to the formation of new tumors

examples.x: Cancer cells often metastasize, spreading to other parts of the body.
The disease is currently metastasizing, posing a significant threat to the patient's health.
If not addressed early, the issue may metastasize into a larger crisis.
By the time of diagnosis, the disease will have metastasized extensively, making treatment more complex.
During the study, researchers observed how the virus was metastasizing in the laboratory setting.

sense.definition: to die as a result of a disease or injury

examples.x: He succumbed to his injuries after the car accident.
By the time the ambulance arrived, she had already succumbed to her injuries.
Despite the best efforts of the medical team, he succumbed to the virus.
The patient is succumbing to the disease despite the doctors' efforts.
The patient eventually succumbed to the severe illness despite the treatment.

sense.definition: to have an illness or disease

examples.x: The child suffered from a high fever and cough, prompting his parents to take him to the doctor.
As she watched her loved one suffer from cancer, she felt a deep sense of helplessness and despair.
Access to quality healthcare is crucial for those who suffer from chronic illnesses, ensuring timely diagnosis and effective treatment.
The elderly man suffered from arthritis, finding it increasingly challenging to perform simple tasks like tying his shoes.

sense.definition: to transmit a disease to another person or animal, which makes them infected with the same disease

examples.x: Individuals who fail to follow proper hygiene practices may transfer the virus to their colleagues.
Mosquitoes can transfer diseases like malaria by biting an infected person and then biting another individual.
During a pandemic, it is crucial to avoid close contact to reduce the risk of transferring the virus.
Pets can act as carriers and transfer certain zoonotic diseases to humans if proper precautions are not taken.
Parents are often concerned about children transferring respiratory infections within school environments.

sense.definition: to eject what has been eaten or drunk through the mouth

examples.x: She sometimes vomits after eating certain foods.
The patient often vomits as a side effect of the medication.
Yesterday, he unexpectedly vomited after having dinner at the restaurant.
After the roller coaster ride, some people vomited due to motion sickness.
Right now, she is feeling nauseous and might be vomiting soon.

sense.definition: to make a disease or medical condition worse or more serious

examples.x: Ignoring medication can aggravate the symptoms of a chronic illness.
Smoking can aggravate respiratory conditions like asthma.
Skipping physical therapy sessions may aggravate a recovering injury.
It aggravated the injury when proper care was not taken.
Ignoring early signs of infection can aggravate the progression of diseases.

sense.definition: to get sick, usually with bacteria or a virus

examples.x: Be careful around him; you don't want to catch the flu.
Be careful when visiting the hospital, so you don't catch an infection.
Children in school often catch and spread germs quickly.
Many people caught the virus during the winter.
The crowded train is a place where you can easily catch a cold.

sense.definition: to get infected by a disease or virus

examples.x: Despite taking precautions, he still contracted pneumonia after being exposed to the virus during flu season.
She contracted HIV from her partner who had not disclosed their status, leading to a lifelong struggle with the virus.
Many individuals unknowingly contract Lyme disease from tick bites while spending time outdoors.
During the pandemic, numerous people contracted COVID-19 after coming into contact with infected individuals in crowded spaces.
Despite efforts to prevent transmission, some individuals still contract hepatitis C.

sense.definition: to push air out of our mouth with a sudden noise

examples.x: Don't cough into your hand; it's better to use a tissue.
He had to cough to clear his throat.
Please cover your mouth when you cough.
She was coughing so much that she had to leave the room.
When he began to cough during his speech, someone offered him a glass of water.

sense.definition: to start to have a particular disease or problem

examples.x: Despite leading a healthy lifestyle, he developed high blood pressure in his late fifties.
After years of smoking, she developed lung cancer and had to undergo extensive treatment.
As she aged, she developed arthritis, experiencing pain and stiffness in her joints.
Poor dental hygiene led him to develop cavities and gum disease, requiring dental intervention.
Long-term exposure to sunlight without protection caused her to develop skin cancer in her forties.

sense.definition: to make someone physically sick or to cause mental trouble
to throw up the contents of the stomach, often due to illness or nausea
to state that one feels ill or one's body part is in pain

examples.x: She came to the doctor's office to complain of persistent headaches that had been troubling her for weeks.
The customer called the helpline to complain of poor service received during their recent shopping experience.
He decided to complain of unfair treatment at work after realizing he was being consistently overlooked for promotions.
The residents gathered at the town hall meeting to complain of the worsening traffic congestion in their neighborhood.
Despite complaining of stomach pain, she insisted on finishing the marathon, determined to cross the finish line.

sense.definition: to lose a large amount of fluid through urinating, vomiting, or intense physical activity

examples.x: After running a marathon in the hot sun without adequate hydration, he began to dehydrate.
The athlete pushed herself to her limits during the intense training session, causing her to dehydrate rapidly.
During his illness, he became nauseous and began to vomit, causing him to dehydrate.
Despite the hot weather, he neglected to drink enough water while working outside, leading him to dehydrate.
The medication he was prescribed had a side effect of increasing urination, causing him to dehydrate.

sense.definition: (of an illness or injury) to confine someone to bed
to lose consciousness

examples.x: It was so hot in the room that she felt like she was going to pass out.
He passed out from exhaustion after the marathon.
After standing for hours in the sun, he passed out from dehydration.
She felt dizzy and nearly passed out during the presentation.
Without enough ventilation in the room, several people started to feel like they might pass out.
During the boxing match, a strong punch made him pass out momentarily.
She hit her head against the shelf and passed out instantly.

sense.definition: to have or exhibit particular signs or symptoms during a medical check-up

examples.x: The patient presents with a persistent cough and fever, indicating a possible respiratory infection.
Upon examination, the child presents with swollen glands and a sore throat, suggestive of a viral illness.
The elderly woman presents with joint pain and stiffness, suggestive of arthritis.
The athlete presents with muscle weakness and fatigue, indicative of overtraining.

sense.definition: to become sick again after an improvement in one's health
to blow air out of our nose and mouth in a sudden way

examples.x: Don't forget to cover your mouth when you sneeze.
I sneeze uncontrollably when I have a cold.
If you sneeze into your hand, remember to wash it afterwards.
She began to sneeze due to the dust in the room.
Whenever I dust my house, I sneeze a lot.

sense.definition: to suddenly lose consciousness from a lack of oxygen in the brain, which is caused by a shock, etc.

examples.x: Prolonged standing in the heat can cause some individuals to faint due to dehydration.
The sight of blood can be distressing for some people and may cause them to faint.
She often faints at the sight of needles, making medical appointments challenging.
Last night, he unexpectedly fainted during the scary movie.
